data.title: Anthropic continued to push model boundaries with latest Claude Sonnet 4.6 unveiling
data.description:
Fresh off its $30B funding round that valued it at $380B, Anthropic (ANTHRO) unveiled its Claude Sonnet 4.6 artificial intelligence model on Tuesday, its second release in two weeks.

In a statement on its website, Anthropic said Claude Sonnet 4.6 has several improvements in various areas, such as coding, long-context reasoning, agent planning, knowledge work, and design. It also has a 1M token context window in beta, the company added.

"Sonnet 4.6 brings much-improved coding skills to more of our users," Anthropic, which is backed by Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and others, said. "Improvements in consistency, instruction following, and more have made developers with early access prefer Sonnet 4.6 to its predecessor by a wide margin. They often even prefer it to our smartest model from November 2025, Claude Opus 4.5."

"Performance that would have previously required reaching for an Opus-class model—including on real-world, economically valuable office tasks—is now available with Sonnet 4.6," Anthropic added. "The model also shows a major improvement in computer use skills compared to prior Sonnet models."

Anthropic added that Claude Sonnet 4.6 is available now on all Claude plans, Claude Cowork, Claude Code, the company's API, and all major cloud platforms. It can also be used by default on its free Claude tier, which has file creation, connectors, skills, and compaction.

Claude Opus 4.6 was released by Anthropic earlier this month.

data.title: Uber and Life360 to allow for greater platform integration
data.description:
• To make it easier for families to coordinate ridesharing and track loved ones in real-time, Uber (UBER) is expanding its partnership with Life360 (LIF) by enabling greater integration between platforms. 
  • Over the coming months, Uber (UBER) and Life360 (LIF) will deliver a more comprehensive app that can track trips in real time, allow for ride booking, and enable Life360 members to link with their Uber (UBER) accounts and vice versa.
  • “By linking Uber accounts with Life360, we’re creating a one-stop experience where families can stay informed when loved ones are on the move—especially parents of teens as they coordinate rides and track trips to school, activities, and more,” said Margarita Peker, Uber’s global head of Family Growth.
  • The Uber/Life360 integration will be available later this year.

data.title: USA Compression forecasts $770M–$800M adjusted EBITDA in 2026 while integrating J-W Power acquisition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: USA Compression Partners, LP (USAC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Micah Green, President & CEO, stated the company closed the J-W Power acquisition on January 12, expanding USA Compression’s reach across all major U.S. oil and gas basins and horsepower classes. He emphasized, “With this transaction, we are leaning into the USA Compression name with broader reach all across this great country.”
  • Green reported high operational performance, noting a “TRIR of 0.39, which is approximately half of the industry average,” and highlighted record results: “We delivered full year adjusted EBITDA of $613.8 million and DCF of $385.7 million, both are records for the company.”
  • The CEO pointed to strong utilization: “We maintained high average utilization in excess of 94% throughout the year and ended the year at 94.5%.”
  • Green described refinancing activities that reduced borrowing costs and increased strategic flexibility, and noted the company’s resilience through leadership changes, a new headquarters, a shared services model, and a new ERP platform.
  • Christopher Wauson, Senior VP & COO, stated, “Since the close of the transaction on January 12, we have begun planning to optimize route management, inventory, contracts and operational structures to begin to realize synergies as early as this year,” and outlined anticipated $10 million to $20 million in annual run rate synergies by the end of 2027.
  • Wauson reported, “We have already begun moving contracts under USA Compression MSAs, and we'll work to extend average contract duration throughout this year.”
  • The COO addressed the addition of approximately 200,000 idle horsepower from J-W, estimating about 50,000 horsepower is readily deployable, with the remainder under review for potential monetization.
  • Christopher Paulsen, Senior VP, CFO & Treasurer, stated, “In Q4, we increased pricing to an all-time high, averaging $21.69 per horsepower, a 1% increase in sequential quarters and a 4% increase compared to the year ago period.”
  • Paulsen outlined, “Our fourth quarter adjusted gross margins came in at 66.8%, right on historical trend.”

Outlook

  • Paulsen provided guidance for 2026: “We are forecasting adjusted EBITDA of $770 million to $800 million and distributable cash flow of $480 million to $510 million.”
  • Maintenance capital is forecasted at $60 million to $70 million, with expansion capital ranging from $230 million to $250 million, including just over 100,000 new horsepower and upgrades for improved telemetry practices.
  • Paulsen highlighted, “The net result of our budget should enable us to improve upon our debt metrics with our near-term targets at 3.75x debt-to-EBITDA, a quarter-turn improvement over the next 12 months.”
  • Management expects synergies from the J-W acquisition to be realized by 2027 and plans for “modest onetime cost associated with the transaction in 2026.”

Financial Results

  • Paulsen reported, “Our fourth quarter 2025 net income was $27.8 million, operating income was $76.6 million, net cash provided by operating activities was $139.5 million and cash interest expense net was $43.4 million.”
  • He stated, “Our total fleet horsepower at the end of the quarter was approximately 3.9 million horsepower, adding approximately 21,000 horsepower as compared to the prior quarter.”
  • Expansion capital expenditures for Q4 were $40 million and maintenance capital was $7.8 million. For the full year, adjusted EBITDA reached $613.8 million, and distributable cash flow was $385.7 million, “above the recently increased guidance, in part due to the final preferred unit conversion in December.”

Q&A

  • Douglas Irwin, Citigroup: Asked for a breakdown of the $250 million growth budget and its run rate moving forward, as well as the impact of long lead times. Paulsen responded, “About $205 million of growth capital is tied in with the typical compression business, both new units, make ready and reconfigurations...approximately $150 million of that $205 million is tied to new units.” He noted long lead times, especially for larger units, but cited the flexibility provided by the J-W manufacturing business.
  • Irwin: Asked about balance sheet improvements and potential for distribution growth. Paulsen replied, “Our normalized number is about 1.55x on Q4. We're looking for that number to be in the 1.6x plus range next year or this coming year that is. And as that number starts to expand beyond 1.6x...we need to continue to have conversations with all of our unitholders as to what the right answer is in terms of distribution growth.”
  • James Rollyson, Raymond James: Inquired about the timing of the 105,000 capacity adds. Green stated, “I think most of what's coming on this year is in the back half of the year, July forward.” Wauson added, “The bulk of the horsepower comes in late Q3 into Q4.”
  • Rollyson: Asked about lead times and equipment cost trends. Green replied, “The main driver in the lead times is Caterpillar engines and the data center demand for generation has driven that lead time out...I expect we'll see some type of increase at some point this year. I haven't heard of one yet. But I'm sure 1 will come down later on this year.”
  • Gabriel Moreen, Mizuho: Asked about the distributed power space. Green indicated they have evaluated opportunities but have not pursued any yet, continuing to monitor for suitable models.
  • Moreen: Sought clarification on placing the remaining 50% of new HP for 2026. Wauson expressed confidence in contracting the balance soon.
  • Nate Pendleton, Texas Capital Bank: Asked about long-term horsepower growth strategy amid long lead times. Wauson said, “It's not going to affect our 2026 growth, but in 2027, we are working to secure that and figure that out,” with the J-W manufacturing business providing optionality.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on capital allocation, timing, lead times, pricing trends, and distribution policy, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarity and confirmation of growth plans.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, repeatedly referencing operational strength, integration benefits, and future prospects. Phrases such as “We are confident” and “We look forward to creating unitholder value” indicated optimism. During Q&A, management acknowledged challenges but emphasized flexibility and proactive planning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted toward greater strategic ambition with the J-W acquisition and a more explicit focus on integration synergies and expanded manufacturing capabilities. Analysts’ tone remained consistent, focused on growth visibility and capital efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more detailed, with specific adjusted EBITDA and DCF targets, compared to the previous quarter’s preliminary outlook.
  • Strategic focus has shifted to integration of J-W Power, with explicit synergy targets and expanded manufacturing capabilities, contrasting with the prior quarter’s emphasis on organic growth and cost savings.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on capacity additions, pricing, and capital deployment, but the latest call included more questions about manufacturing lead times and strategic flexibility.
  • Management’s confidence level increased, emphasizing new opportunities from the acquisition and flexibility in addressing supply chain challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited increased lead times for new equipment—now over two years—as a significant challenge, with plans to mitigate through the J-W manufacturing business and internal planning.
  • The integration of 200,000 idle horsepower from J-W poses utilization and monetization risks, with management stating, “We will analyze the best path, including potential monetization of a portion of the horsepower.”
  • Expansion capital plans and onetime costs associated with the acquisition could affect short-term margins, though management expects margin alignment and synergy realization by 2027.

Final Takeaway

USA Compression Partners closed the transformative J-W Power acquisition, setting ambitious targets for 2026 with forecasted adjusted EBITDA of $770 million to $800 million and a focus on integration synergies, expanded manufacturing capabilities, and operational flexibility. Management emphasized confidence in maintaining strong utilization, improving debt metrics, and capturing new growth opportunities across key U.S. basins, while actively managing risks related to lead times and asset deployment as they pursue sustained unitholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: NESR outlines path to $2B revenue run rate by end of 2026 while expanding MENA contract base
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: National Energy Services Reunited Corp. (NESR) (NESR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Sherif Foda, Executive Chairman & CEO, stated that NESR "ended a tremendous '25 with even stronger-than-expected results in the fourth quarter" and highlighted the successful launch of "the largest unconventional frac program in sector history," cost management, and achieving record revenue and free cash flow. Foda added, "these results are a testament to our hard-working teams in the field, their dedication and commitment."
  • The CEO emphasized NESR's position in the MENA region, referencing robust investment cycles in Kuwait, where upstream spending is expected to be $8 billion to $10 billion per year through 2030, and the transformation of Kuwait into NESR's second largest market. Foda explained, "the evolution NESR to Kuwait over the coming years will dovetail perfectly with the transformation of the Kuwait energy sector."
  • Foda also pointed to significant opportunities in North Africa, notably Libya, where new collaborations and bid rounds are expected to increase oil capacity and NESR is positioned to play a key role in capacity expansion.
  • The CEO called the start-up of the Jafurah frac project "the most anticipated topic of this call," confirming operations began on time and stating, "cost control across both procurement and operation is evident in the fourth quarter result, and there is much more optimization to achieve."
  • Stefan Angeli, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "our fourth quarter revenue was $398.3 million, an all-time high, representing an increase of 34.9% sequentially and 15.9% year-over-year." Angeli further noted that "adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $84.4 million, representing a margin of 21.2%, broadly in line with the third quarter levels."

Outlook

  • Angeli stated, "we continue to see a path to exiting '26 at an annualized revenue run rate of approximately $2 billion, underpinned by our growing contract portfolio and consistent operational delivery."
  • Full year 2026 EBITDA margins are expected "to remain broadly consistent with '25," with gradual sequential improvement in margins through the year.
  • For Q1 2026, interest expense is expected to be approximately $7.5 million, with full year 2026 interest expense anticipated in the $22 million range.
  • Capital expenditures for 2026 are projected at approximately $165 million, with free cash flow conversion expected to be "approximately 35% to 40% conversion from adjusted EBITDA."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenue reached $398.3 million, up 34.9% sequentially and 15.9% year-over-year, driven by mobilization of the Jafurah contract and increased activity in North Africa.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $84.4 million, with a margin of 21.2%.
  • Q4 2025 included $24.1 million of total charges and credits impacting adjusted EBITDA, primarily related to credit loss provisions, impairment charges, restructuring costs, and asset write-offs.
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share for Q4 2025 was $0.32.
  • Full-year 2025 revenue totaled $1.324 billion, full-year adjusted EBITDA was $281.4 million with a 21.3% margin, and full-year adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.81.
  • Cash flow from operations for 2025 was $264.2 million, with free cash flow at $120.8 million.
  • Gross debt at year-end was $310 million, net debt was $185.3 million, and net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio stood at 0.66.

Q&A

  • John Anderson, Barclays Bank PLC, Research Division: Asked about Jafurah's ramp-up and how to achieve the $2 billion run rate. Foda responded that by Q2, NESR expects to be in steady state, with additional fleet additions possible in Q3 and Q4, stating, "the run rate for our stages per quota should be seen very clearly in Q3."
  • Anderson also inquired about supply chain management. Foda explained, "we planned it from very early beginning to ensure that we have local sand, adequate supplies... all this is basically solved."
  • Saurabh Pant, BofA Securities, Research Division: Questioned incremental efficiencies and margin profile at Jafurah. Foda described cost optimizations and the use of AI for maintenance, projecting that "we believe that we can do more stages per day... another 20% efficiency... will definitely have an impact on our margins."
  • Joshua Silverstein, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: Asked about CapEx and technology investments. Foda outlined planned CapEx of $150 million to $180 million and discussed NESR's VC-style investments to commercialize new technologies.
  • Derek Podhaizer, Piper Sandler & Co., Research Division: Sought clarity on Saudi business outside Jafurah. Foda confirmed activity ramp-up and added, "rigs are ramping up... Aramco is going to definitely provide some more color... rigs are coming back."
  • Sherif Elmaghrabi, BTIG, LLC, Research Division: Asked about Oman. Foda clarified that recent restructuring costs were one-time and related to a new contract, not asset redeployment, and explained opportunities in Syria and Libya.
  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: Asked about critical minerals. Foda stated, "the lithium that was put on the display was NESR lithium... first mover in -- with -- in that business."
  • Jeffrey Robertson, Water Tower Research LLC: Inquired about pipeline tenders and margin profile. Foda stated, "the company will remain with the same margin level that it is today," and noted that most awards will be in 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on growth drivers, margin sustainability, supply chain management, and regional expansion, maintaining a positive to slightly positive tone, particularly regarding Jafurah and new contract opportunities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, repeatedly referencing "record results," "solid floor of activity," and "very excited and optimistic" about growth prospects. Foda signaled confidence in both prepared remarks and Q&A, especially regarding operational execution and margin maintenance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the management tone was more assertive about exceeding prior targets and the scale of opportunities, while analysts continued to probe for details around execution and risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was reiterated and strengthened, with emphasis on achieving a $2 billion revenue run rate versus the previous quarter's reference to this as a target.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward doubling company size and leveraging high regional investment cycles, compared to the prior quarter's emphasis on contract wins and Jafurah's operational start.
  • Analysts maintained interest in growth prospects and execution risk, but questions in the current quarter probed deeper into margin sustainability, supply chain resilience, and technology commercialization.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, adjusted EBITDA, free cash flow, and net debt all improved sequentially, demonstrating execution against previously stated goals.
  • Management's confidence and forward-looking statements increased, as did the specificity of market and contract updates.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged one-time items impacting adjusted EBITDA, including credit loss provisions, impairment charges, and restructuring costs, but stated, "we do not expect any material contract mobilization-related restructuring costs in 2026."
  • Supply chain challenges were addressed in depth, with Foda stating, "we solved all the issues of any kind of blockade from tariff, supply chain, logistics, whatever."
  • The company acknowledged that margin pressures remain, especially due to competitively priced contract wins, but expressed confidence in ongoing cost control and operational discipline.
  • Analysts asked about exposure to new markets like Syria and Libya, with management noting ongoing evaluation of payment and security risks before entering.

Final Takeaway

Management conveyed that NESR is entering 2026 from a position of strength, with record financial performance, robust free cash flow, and a clear path to a $2 billion revenue run rate by year-end. The company emphasized its ability to scale, maintain margins, and capitalize on high-growth opportunities across the MENA region, supported by disciplined execution, strong customer relationships, and a growing pipeline of multi-year contracts. With a strategy focused on both organic and technology-driven expansion, NESR sees sustainable long-term value creation as it leverages the region’s unique investment climate and demand for upstream energy services.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Robinhood nears launch of private markets fund for retail investors
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) introduced the Robinhood Ventures Fund I (RVI), Robinhood Venture's inaugural fund that's expected to start trading on the New York Stock Exchange in coming weeks, the company said Tuesday.

After its initial public offering, the closed-end fund is expected to trade under the RVI ticker. Robinhood (HOOD) customers can request IPO shares of RVI at an expected price of $25 per share. 

RVI is designed to provide exposure to a concentrated portfolio of private companies, including Airwallex, Boom, Databricks, Mercor, Oura, Ramp, and Revolut, with additional companies expected to be added over time. The fund has entered into an agreement to buy shares of Stripe (STRIP) that Robinhood expects to close after RVI's IPO.

The new fund is part of the company's mission to democratize markets. It comes as the number of publicly traded companies has declined. Meanwhile, companies are staying private longer and are increasing in both number and value. Robinhood said there are 6.5 times as many private companies as public, with U.S. private companies' total value exceeding $10T, according to data from Apollo Academy and the Federal Reserve.

"Opening up private markets will resolve one of the greatest longstanding inequities in capital markets today, and we’re excited to bring these opportunities to all with Robinhood Ventures Fund I," said Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev.

The new fund is designed to be accessible to all investors, with no accreditation requirements, no investment minimums, a competitive management fee, no performance fees, and daily liquidity as a publicly traded fund on the NYSE.

RVI pays its investment adviser, Robinhood Ventures, a management fee at an annual rate of 2.00% of net assets, calculated and payable quarterly. The management fee, however, is reduced to 1.00% for the first six months immediately following RVI's initial public offering. The fund does not have a performance fee.

Robinhood (HOOD) shares rose 0.9% in Tuesday afternoon trading.

data.title: Bristol Myers wins FDA priority review for multiple myeloma therapy
data.description:
• Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) announced on Tuesday that the FDA has granted priority review for a marketing application targeting U.S. approval of iberdomide, its experimental therapy for multiple myeloma, a blood cancer.
  • The regulator has assigned August 17, 2026, as the target action date for Bristol Myers’ (BMY) New Drug Application, which seeks the use of iberdomide as part of a combination regimen for those with relapsed or refractory MM.
  • The Princeton, New Jersey-based firm has backed the NDA with data from a planned review of its Phase 3 EXCALIBER-RRMM study, which is currently underway targeting R/R MM patients.
  • The company stated that the filing is based on an endpoint related to minimal residual disease, which has become a sensitive and clinically important measure for evaluating disease response in MM therapy.

data.title: Veeva Systems upgraded at Morgan Stanley as selloff reflects CRM risks
data.description:
Morgan Stanley on Tuesday upgraded Veeva Systems (VEEV) to Equal Weight from Underweight, noting that the provider of customer relationship management ((CRM)) software better reflects its market risks after its recent sell-off.

Analyst Craig Hettenbach pointed out that Veeva (VEEV) has fallen 45% since its recent peak in October. He attributed the sell-off to concerns over AI-related disruption in the software sector and the company’s recent performance in the CRM market, where it has lost four of the top 10 pharma companies.

“The pendulum has swung too far from overenthusiasm to extreme pessimism,” Hettenbach wrote, adding that “while there are lingering risks from share loss in CRM,” VEEV better reflects the concerns as its stock trades at a 10-year low on an EV/FCF basis.

The analyst highlighted VEEV's financial position, including its $6.6B in cash, and called the company's inaugural share repurchase program, announced in January, a “potential support” for the stock.

However, Hettenbach lowered his price target on Veeva (VEEV) to $205 from $222, assuming a 2x turn decline in EV/FCF to recognize multiple compressions in the software sector. The analyst projected VEEV to provide in-line guidance with a “slight upside” for Q4 2025 when it releases quarterly financials on Mar. 4.

data.title: What's next for Cisco after its post-earnings stock slide?
data.description:
Cisco Systems (CSCO) tumbled more than 12% following its Q2 earnings report, despite the networking giant delivering a record $15.35B in revenue and year-over-year top-line growth of 10%. While the company exceeded Wall Street’s expectations on its headline numbers and raised its full-year guidance, investors reacted negatively to narrowing gross margins and the potential for continued profitability pressure.

With the sharp post-earnings selloff, analyst sentiment has become a battleground between those focused on a massive AI-driven infrastructure cycle and those wary of rising component costs.

What Do Seeking Alpha Analysts Say About Cisco’s Future?

Analyst expectations for Cisco are currently divided, reflecting a mix of optimism regarding the “AI-era” transition and skepticism over near-term margin headwinds.

Bulls pointed to the company’s massive inflection point in AI infrastructure, noting that orders from hyperscalers reached $2.1B in the second quarter alone. This figure matched the total AI orders for all of fiscal year 2025.

Optimists also highlighted the ongoing campus networking refresh cycle, driven by the end-of-support for legacy products and the rapid adoption of Wi-Fi 7. These analysts believe Cisco is “quietly winning” the AI buildout through its Silicon One architecture and optics portfolio, which are seeing triple-digit growth in specific segments like Acacia.

On the other side of the spectrum, skeptics highlighted the immediate pressure on profitability as non-GAAP gross margins fell 120 basis points to 67.5%. This decline was largely attributed to soaring memory costs and a shifting product mix, which management expects will continue to weigh on margins in the third quarter.

Additionally, some analysts expressed concern over the high valuation multiples relative to growth, pointing out that Cisco’s PEG ratio remains less attractive than faster-growing peers like Arista Networks (ANET).

There is also a shared concern regarding the acquisition of Splunk, as its transition to a cloud-based subscription model is currently creating a drag on revenue growth that may not abate until late in the 2026 fiscal year.

Here’s a breakdown of what some analysts had to say:

  • Steven Fiorillo, Rating: Strong Buy: “Cisco just achieved record revenue, accelerating orders, and expanding operating leverage as Cisco is positioning the Silicon One and optics stack in addition to the security platform to monetize the next phase of AI buildout… the current valuation doesn’t make sense for a company that has more than 20% EPS growth on the horizon over the next 2 years.” - Cisco Systems: Quietly Winning The AI Buildout, But Nobody’s Pricing It In

  • Justin Purohit, CPA, Rating: Hold: “While I view CSCO’s long-term role in AI infrastructure favorably, I believe better risk-adjusted opportunities exist elsewhere in the market today… gross margin fell 120 basis points in Q2 and is expected to decline further to 65.5%–66.5% in Q3, driven largely by soaring memory costs.” - Cisco Q2 Results: Narrowing Margins Overshadow Earnings Beat, Shares Fairly Valued

  • Vladimir Dimitrov, CFA, Rating: Hold: “Cisco’s investors got a reality check that the company is not a pure growth stock, while it was certainly priced as one… In spite of the strong momentum in Networking, the prospects of margin pressures are overwhelming.” - Cisco Q2 Earnings: The Wrong Way To Get On The AI Bandwagon

  • Gary Gambino, Rating: Hold: “Cisco is seeing increased orders from AI infrastructure demand as well as normal upgrade cycle timing. However, it looks like it will take until Fiscal Q4 2026 for this to really show up in revenue and EPS. This is due in part to memory supply headwinds and Splunk transitioning to a subscription model.” - Cisco Systems: The Market Is Impatient For Growth

What Do the Quant Ratings Say?

Cisco currently carries a Hold rating according to Seeking Alpha’s quant system. While the company scores exceptionally well in profitability, it faces significant headwinds in valuation and overall revenue growth compared to the broader technology sector.

Profitability: Cisco demonstrates world-class efficiency, receiving an A+ grade. The company’s net income margin of 19% is nearly quadruple the sector median of 5%, reflecting high operational performance even amid rising costs.

Valuation: The company earns a D+ grade for valuation, as it appears overstretched relative to peers. Its Price-to-Book ratio of 6.37 is significantly higher than the sector median of 3.55, though its 2.13% dividend yield remains a strong point for income-focused investors.

Growth: Growth metrics remain a concern, earning a D- grade. Forward revenue growth is projected at 6%, which trails the sector median of 10%, though the company’s 37% CAPEX growth indicates it is investing heavily in future infrastructure.

Momentum: Momentum receives a B grade, supported by a 1-year price performance of 20%. Despite the recent post-earnings dip, the stock has outperformed the broader sector’s 6% decline over the same period.

Earnings Revisions: The revisions category scores a B+, signaling optimism from the analyst community. Over the last 90 days, there have been 20 upward EPS revisions compared to only two downward adjustments, suggesting confidence in the company’s ability to exceed its conservative guidance.

data.title: Mississippi to hold hearing on xAI data center amid pollution lawsuit threat: report
data.description:
On Tuesday, Mississippi regulators will host a public hearing over plans by xAI (X.AI) to develop a gas-fired power plant to power its Colossus II data center, Reuters reported.

The AI startup faces a lawsuit threat by the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People, or NAACP, accusing the company of violating federal law by installing and operating gas turbines without air permits, the report added.

NAACP, acting on behalf of the largely African American local community living near the existing Colossus I facility in Memphis, Tennessee, noted that xAI started illegally installing and then operating 27 gas turbines at a site in Southaven, Mississippi, to power Colossus II, sited across the state line in Memphis, the report noted.

The organization said that this was being done without properly receiving the required preconstruction or operating air permits under the Clean Air Act.

xAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

"Pollution from these turbines is worsening and will continue to worsen the already poor air quality in Southaven, Mississippi and the Memphis metropolitan area," said the NAACP in a letter declaring its intent to sue xAI, according to the report.

The NAACP noted that the turbines have the potential to release a significant amount of smog-forming nitrogen oxides (NOx), above the Clean Air Act’s “major source” threshold, and other pollutants, including fine particulate matter and carcinogenic formaldehyde, harming largely African American local communities, the report added.

The Clean Air Act requires plaintiffs to indicate their intent to sue 60 days in advance.

Elon Musk's xAI has been building its Colossus supercomputer, which is being used to train the company's AI chatbot Grok in Memphis and expanding to Southaven. It is currently in phase 2 and is looking to start a third phase soon, the report noted.

Following the Southern Environmental Law Center, or SELC, which is representing the NAACP in this new lawsuit threat, filing an intent to sue xAI in 2024 for installing 35 non-permitted turbines at its Colossus 1 site, the firm removed 20 turbines and received permits for the remaining 15, the report added.

The Mississippi Department of Environmental Quality, or DEQ, hearing is the only chance for the public to discuss the project, which SELC has noted will be the largest new source of pollution in the area around Memphis in years, the report added.

The DEQ said xAI has filed permit applications for 41 permanent turbines at the site and will run some temporary turbines while the applications are being reviewed, the report noted.

data.title: DTE Energy nears deal to serve second major Michigan data center, CEO says
data.description:
DTE Energy (DTE) shares are little changed in Tuesday's trading after reporting better-than-expected Q4 adjusted earnings and saying it expects to finalize a new data center agreement in Michigan in the coming weeks.

DTE Energy (DTE) is making "great progress" toward reaching an agreement with an undisclosed data center customer and is expecting to finalize a deal "in the coming weeks," President and CEO Joi Harris said on the company's earnings conference call.

The utility said it negotiated its first hyperscale data center contracts in Q4 to provide 1.4 GW of load to power Oracle's new data center in Saline Township, Michigan, which it said would create $300M in annual benefits for its existing customers, and has another 3 GW of load in advanced discussions that could be finalized in the coming weeks.

The CEO also said the company's data center contracts would be structured so that existing customers would not be burdened with the cost of powering them.

DTE (DTE) raised its five-year spending plan by 20% to $36.5B, up from the $30B it expected in its 2025-29 plan, intended primarily to serve data center customers.

The company reported Q4 adjusted earnings of $1.65/share, above the $1.52/share FactSet consensus estimate; adjusted profit from its electric segment rose 8.2% Y/Y to $211M, and its gas segment profit jumped 16.3% to $121M.
